content.text: What

A modular microfrontend (MFE) that any part of the Phenom platform can embed to create or edit an interview request. Each section of the form is independently togglable via configuration props so consuming surfaces can expose a simple or advanced scheduling experience as needed.
Why
HRM and CRM today carry parallel interview-request UIs; every new scheduling feature risks duplicate UI work and inconsistent UX.
By owning the UI layer as a shared MFE, Scheduling can ship features end-to-end and give every surface the same configurable building blocks.
A modular, configurable MFE enables consistent interview creation UX across CRM, HRM, and scheduling-link flows while allowing each surface to tailor defaults.
Positions Scheduling as the system of record for interview-request UI across the platform, reducing long-term maintenance fragmentation.
Success Metrics
CRM request creation and edit flows embed the MFE with agreed default section configuration and no critical regressions versus current scheduling behavior.
HRM request creation and edit flows embed the MFE with parity to prior HRM interview-request UX for in-scope sections.
Scheduling link creation embeds the MFE with agreed defaults.
New scheduling features ship inside the MFE without requiring duplicate UI stories from every consuming team for the same capability.
MFE renders correctly in full-width (web) contexts used by Phase 1 surfaces.
Requirements
Phase 1 scope (in this epic)
CRM: request creation and edit flows embedding the MFE.
HRM: request creation and edit flows embedding the MFE.
Scheduling link creation embedding the MFE.
Form sections (each independently shown or hidden via config)
Session Details
— Title, Duration, Description (new feature).
Scorecard
— Scorecard selection, Preview.
Participants Selection
— Type-ahead search, Interview teams configuration, Hiring team quick add.
Meeting Channel
— Channel selection, Secondary channel, Meeting Intelligence toggle.
Communications
— Candidate availability, Candidate invite, Interview invite.
Reminders and Confirmations
— Interview, candidate, and requester reminders; Confirmation flow.
Availability Configuration
— Within X days, Specific slots, Buffer time, Meeting time zone, Time-slot interval, Block time (before/after).
Additional Options
— Candidate reschedule toggle, Candidate proposal toggle, Candidate reschedule window, Private invite toggle.
Viewport / responsive behavior (Phase 1)
Full-width layout: standard web desktop experience for CRM, HRM, and scheduling link surfaces.
Integration
MFE embeddable in CRM and HRM request flows and in scheduling link creation.
Configuration API (props/events) documented for consuming teams; default section presets per surface tracked in child stories.
Supports create and edit modes where applicable (CRM/HRM requests).
MFE framework/architecture — Engineering alignment (see Open Questions).
Cross-Team Dependencies
CRM | YES | Embed MFE in request create/edit; align default section configuration | see child stories
HRM | YES | Embed MFE in request create/edit; align default section configuration | see child stories
Scheduling / Platform | YES | MFE delivery, scheduling link surface, API consumption | PHEM-1880297
Chrome Extension | Phase 2 | Narrow-width embedding and extension shell |
Out of Scope
Chrome Extension and extension-specific UX — tracked in epic PHEM-1949721.
Native mobile apps (iOS/Android) — future consideration.
Wholesale replacement of the legacy CRM scheduling wizard in one cutover (this epic delivers shared MFE infrastructure and Phase 1 embeds; migration strategy may be incremental).
Backend API changes unless explicitly required to support MFE (MFE otherwise consumes existing Scheduling APIs).
Open Questions
What MFE framework/architecture? (Module Federation, single-spa, other) | Eng | Open
How should the MFE handle auth context when embedded in different product shells? | Eng | Open
Is a kick-off with UX and Engineering needed before build? | PM | Open
Chrome Extension in this epic? | PM | Closed — no; use PHEM-1949721.
Documentation
Figma reference (CRM form): https://www.figma.com/design/gMlJjsnD7bfgvwNxA06QzU/Scheduling---Hybrid-Meeting-Types-for-Interviews?node-id=44-7692
Phase 2 epic: PHEM-1949721
Visual QA: TBD
Release Documentation: TBD
